title: Migrate to STRICT mTLS through PERMISSIVE first
description: Roll mTLS out with PERMISSIVE mode to accept both plaintext and mTLS, verify all callers use mTLS, then flip to STRICT.
body: Use when enforcing mutual TLS between meshed services. Do not flip a namespace to STRICT before every client sends mTLS (you will break plaintext callers). Actions: set PeerAuthentication to PERMISSIVE, confirm via telemetry that connections are mTLS (not plaintext), ensure DestinationRule trafficPolicy.tls mode is ISTIO_MUTUAL where needed, then set STRICT; handle non-mesh clients via explicit exceptions. Validation signals include telemetry showing 100% mTLS before cutover, STRICT rejecting plaintext, and mesh calls succeeding with workload certs. Risk boundary: setting STRICT on a production namespace can sever non-mesh clients and is human-gated.
